response: Japan, with a population of 125 million, boasts a robust GDP of $4.2 trillion, though its economy grew modestly by 1.1% last year. The country maintains a low unemployment rate of 2.6% and faces an inflation rate of 3.2%, while its public debt stands at a staggering 263% of GDP. With a high life expectancy of 84.6 years, Japan also ranks among the world’s top exporters, shipping goods worth $756 billion annually.
